summary: Iteration 69: Branch-specific filtering bug fixes VERIFIED. All 16 backend tests passed. Endpoints correctly accept branch_id parameter for movements, orders, capital-history, vendors. Frontend correctly passes currentBranch.id to all history fetch calls and saves prices to branch_prices when on a branch.

BUG_1_CENTRALIZED_HISTORY.description: Capital History, Movement History, and Order History were centralized (showed all branches) instead of branch-specific
implementation: GET /api/products/{id}/orders?branch_id=xxx filters both POs and sales by branch_id (line 751-801)
Frontend passes currentBranch.id to fetchMovements (line 75), fetchOrders (line 84), fetchCapitalHistory (line 93)

BUG_2_RETAIL_PRICE_ALL_BRANCHES.description: Changing retail price on one branch affected all other branches
implementation: handleSave (line 105-141) now saves to /branch-prices/{id} when currentBranch is set
Only non-price fields (name, category, etc.) are saved to global product
Branch-specific prices stored in branch_prices collection

BUG_4_CAPITAL_CHANGES_BRANCH_ID.description: Capital changes needed to include branch_id for tracking

critical_code_review_comments: All endpoints correctly implemented with Optional[str] branch_id parameter
Backward compatibility maintained in capital-history: $or query includes records without branch_id
Vendor filtering uses $or to include branch-specific AND global vendors (empty/missing branch_id)
Frontend correctly passes currentBranch.id to all relevant API calls
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 69 verified all 4 bug fixes for Product Detail page branch-specific filtering: (1) Movement/Order/Capital history endpoints now accept branch_id query param, (2) Frontend handleSave saves prices to branch_prices when on a branch, (3) Vendors are now branch-specific with filtering in product detail, (4) Capital changes from POs and transfers include branch_id. All tests passed. Database has existing data from production deployment.
